# Action Plan

## Week 1

- Schedule a family meeting with Sarah's parents to discuss attendance issues and develop a home study plan.
- Arrange for a mentor or tutor to meet with Sarah once a week to provide academic support and help her catch up on missed work.

## Week 2

- Implement after-school tutoring sessions twice a week to reinforce learning and address homework challenges.
- Begin individualized instruction sessions with Sarah to identify specific areas where she needs extra attention.

## Week 3

- Conduct behavior management training for both Sarah and other students in the class.
- Set up a peer mediation program to resolve conflicts between Sarah and her classmates.

## Week 4

- Organize a parent-teacher conference to review Sarah's progress and set new goals for improvement.
- Continue with active learning strategies in class to enhance engagement and participation.

## Sources Used

- Family Engagement
- Mentoring/Tutoring
- After-School Opportunities
- Individualized Instruction
- Active Learning

# Action Plan

## Week 1

- Schedule a family meeting with Lily's parents to discuss attendance issues and develop a home study plan.
- Arrange for a mentor or tutor to meet with Lily once a week to provide academic support and monitor her progress.

## Week 2

- Implement after-school tutoring sessions twice a week to help Lily catch up on missed homework.
- Begin individualized instruction sessions three times a week to address specific learning needs identified during meetings.

## Week 3

- Continue after-school tutoring sessions twice a week.
- Conduct one-on-one mentoring sessions with Lily every other day to reinforce positive behavior changes.

## Week 4

- Increase after-school tutoring frequency to four times a week.
- Expand individualized instruction to include more advanced topics to challenge Lily academically.

## Sources Used

- Family Engagement
- Mentoring/Tutoring
- After-School Opportunities
- Individualized Instruction
- Active Learning
